text_en:
[Art. 1] God hath created all men free and equal, and endowed 
them with certain inalienable rights; among which are life and liberty, 
the right of acquiring, possessing and protecting property, and of pur- 
suing and obtaining safety and happiness.
text_haw:
[Pauku 1] . Ua^ana mai ke Akua i na kanaka a pau e 
noho paa ole a e noho like hoi ; a ua haa^i mai no hōi 
oia i niaa ppno e pili paa loa ia lakou ; oia hoi, o ke oU» 
o ka noho kedcea kumu ole ia mai^ a me na pono e loik 
niiai ai a e nmluhiia io ai ka waiwa).

text_en:
[Art. 3] All men may freely speak, write and publish their senti- 
ments on all subjects, being responsible for the abuse of that right; 
and no law shall be passed to restrain or abridge the liberty of speech, 
or of the press.
text_haw:
[Pauku 3] \. Nana no e ae, a e hooia i na Luna mii a 
me(^na Kuhina i hoounaia mai; nana no hoi e hai aku imua 
jo ka Ahaolelo i ke ano o ka noho ana o ke Aupuni i kela 
manawa^ i keia manawa ma kana olelo alii, ae hai aku h6f 
8 
ia lakou i na luea ana e nianao ai he pono na lakoU 
e imi.

text_en:
[Art. 5] The privilege of the writ of Habeas Corpus belongs to all 
men, and shall not be suspended, unless by the King, when, in cases 
of rebellion or invasion, the public safety shall require its suspension.
text_haw:
[Pauku 5] . ka pono o ka palapala kuu kino, no na 
kanilk«i a pau ia; aole hoi e laweia aku ua pono nei. 
Aka, 1 ka manaWa kipi, a kaua paha, i ka Moi wale no 
ka mana e w^eia^i. 
Pai3KU 6. E mau loa no ka pono o ka hookolokolo 
jure ma na inea a pau i like me na mea i hookolokoloia 
pela, ma keia Aupuni, i ka wa i hala akiinei.

text_en:
[Art. 6] The right of trial by jury, in all cases in which it has been 
heretofore used in this Kingdom, shall remain inviolate forever.
text_haw:
[Pauku 6] ?. Ua haawi nui ia, ma keia Kumukanawai, i 
1ca Ahaolelo e kau, i kela manawa i keia manawa, i na 
Kanawai kupono a pau, me ka uku hoopai, a me ka uku 
hoopai ole paba, e like me ko lakou manao he mea e po- 
oo ai kei^ lakuikanaka, a e paa ai, a e maluhia ai ke Au- 
puni; aole nae e kue i keia Kumukanawai.

text_en:
[Art. 7] No person shall be subject to punishment for any offense, 
except on due and legal conviction thereof, in a court having jurisdic- 
tion of the case. 
Digitized by 
Google 
4 CONSTITUTION*
text_haw:
[Pauku 7] . Aole no e hoopaiia kekahi kanaka no ka 
hewa ofeni, ke hookolokolo ole ia mamua iloko o ka Aha- 
hookolokolo kupono i kela hewa^ a hooholoia ka oleio 
hoopai D0 ka hewa, e like me ke Eanawai»

text_en:
[Art. 8] No penoD shall be hold to answer for any crime or offense 
(except in cases of impeachment, or for offenses within the jurisdiction 
of a police or district justice, or in summary proceedings for contempt,) 
unless upon indictment, fully and plainly describing such crime or of- 
fense; and in the trial of any person on the charge of any crime or of- 
fense, he shall have the right to meet the witnesses who are produced 
against him, face to face, to produce witnesses and proofs in his own 
&vor; and by himself, or his counsel, at his election, to examine the 
witnesses produced by himself, and cross-examine those produced 
against him; and to be fully heard in his defense.
text_haw:
[Pauku 8] . Aole e hookolokoloia kekahi kanaka no ke- 
kahi karaima, a ofeni paha ke ole ka palapala hoopii ku- 
pono, e hoomaopopo lea ana i kona karaima, a i kona 
pfeni paha, (koe nae ka hoopii Lunanui a me na ofeni ku- 
pono i ka hookolokoloia iloko o na Ahahookolokolo hoo- 
malu kulanakauhale, a me na Aha o na Lunakanawai 
apana, a me ka hoopai koke no ka hoohaunaele i na Aha- 
hookolokolo,) a ma ka hookolokolo ana i kekahi kanaka 
po kona hewa e ku no ia imua o oa hoike no kona hewa, 
he aio no be ^lo; e hiki hoi ia iu ke kii i na hoike kokua 
i kona uo8^o, a me pa mea e maopopo ai kona pono: a e 
hiki no hoi ia ia, a i kon^^ kokua paha, ina pela kona ma- 
nao, ke ninau i na hoike ana i lawe mai ai, a e ninau no 
hoi i na hoike o kela aoao, a me ka hai aku i na mea e 
inaopopo ai kapono o kona aoao. 
Pavku 9. Ina ua hookolokolo e la kekahi kanaka no 
kekahi ofeni, a ua hoopaiia, a hoopukaia paha mamuli o 
ka palapala hoopii kupono, alaila, aole e hiki ke hookolo- 
kolo hou ia ia no ia hewa hookahi.

text_en:
[Art. 10] No person shall be compelled, in any criminal case to be 
a witness against himself; nor be deprived of life, liberty, or property, 
without due process of law.
text_haw:
[Pauku 10] . Ma ka hookolokolo ana no kekahi karaima, 
'aole e koiia kekahi kanaka e hoike kue ia ia iho; aole hoi 
ia e hoonele ia i ke ola, a i ka malu, a i ka waiwai paha, ke 
ol? i hana pono ia mamuli o ke Kanawai. 
*

text_en:
[Art. 11] No person shall sit as judge or juror, in any case in which 
his relative is interested, either as plaintiff, or defendant, or in the issue 
of which the said judge or juror may have, either directly or through 
a relative, any pecuniary interest.
text_haw:
[Pauku 11] . Aole no e noho kekahi i hinakanawai, a 
i mea Jure pahn, e hookelokolo i kona hoahanau, ina o ka 
Eaea hoopii n hoopiiia paha; aole hoi i ka mea i pili ia ia, 
n i.koua hoahanau paha uia ke ano hui waiwui; a ina i 
hoahewaia, a hookuuia paha kekahi, me ke kue i keia ole- 
lo, alaila, pono no ke aeia e hookolokolo hou ia ia,

text_en:
[Art. 12] Slavery shall, under no circumstances whatever, be tole- 
rated in the Hawaiian Islands: whenever a slave shall enter Hawaiian 
territory he shall be free; no person who imports a slave, or slaves, 
into the King's dominions shall ever enjoy any civil or political rights 
in this realm; but involuntary servitude for the punishment of crime is 
allowable according to law.
text_haw:
[Pauku 12] . Aole loa e noho kauwa kuapaa kekahi ka- 
naka malalō o kekahi ma ko Ijawj»ii nei Pi^e Aina; ina 
hoi e hiki mai kekahi kauwa kuapaa i keia Pae Aina, o ko- 
na kuapaa ole no iu, a o ua mea lawe niai i ke kauwa kna- 
paa iloko o ke Aupuni o ka Moi, e nele no ia i na pono a 
pau o keia Aupuni; aka hoi, aole no e hooleia ka nolio 
kuapaa ana o kekahi, ke hoopaiia oia no ka hewa e liko 
roe ke Kanawai.

text_en:
[Art. 13] Every person has the right to be secure from all unrea- 
sonable searches and seizures of his person, his houses, his papers, and 
effects; and no warrants shall issue, but on probable cause, supported 
by oath or affirmation, and describing the place to be searched, and 
the person or things to be seized.
text_haw:
[Pauku 13] . E kapu no kela kanaka keia kanaka ma 
kona kino, a me kona hale, a me kana mau palapala, u 
me kona waiwai; aole e hopuia, aole hoi e huliia, me ke 
kUmu ole; aole hoi e hoopukaia ka pnhipala kenake mao- 
popo ole ke kumu pono ma ka hoohuoi, a ma ka hoohi- 
ki paha, o ka mea nana i hoopii; a e hoakakaia, iloko o 
ua palapala nei, kahi e huliia a me na kanaka, a mea e ae 
paha e hopuia.

text_en:
[Art. 14] The King conducts His Government for the common good; 
for the protection, safety, prosperity and happiness of His people; and 
not for the profit, honor, or private interest of any one man, family or 
class of men among His subjects. Therefore in making laws for the 
nation, regard shall be had to the protection, interest and welfare not 
only of the King, the Chiefs, and rulers, but of all the people alike. 
Digitized by 
Google
text_haw:
[Pauku 14] . Ke hoomalu nei ke Alii i kona Aupuni no 
ka pono o na kanaka a pau malalo ona; e hoomaluia hoi 
ke ola, a rae ka waiwai, a me ka pomaikai o kona poe ka- 
naka a pau; aole hoi e imiia ka pono, a me ka hano- 
hano, a me ka waiwai o ka mea hookahi a o ka ohana 
hookahi paha, a o ka poe ano hookahi paha o kona poe 
kanaka; a nolaila, i ke kau ana i na Kanawai no keia la- 
huikanaka, e manao nui ia ka maluhia a me ka pomaikai, 
a me ka pono o ka Moi, a me na Lii,-4i me na Luna, a me 
na Makaainana no lioi.

text_en:
[Art. 15] Each member of society has a right to be protected by it 
in the enjoymeat of his life, liberty and property, according to standing 
laws. He is obliged, consequently, to contribute his proportional 
share to the expense of this protection; to give his personal services, 
or an equivalent, when necessary; but no part of the property of any 
individual, can, with justice, be taken from him or applied to public 
uses without his own consent, or that of the King, the Nobles, and tb^ 
Representatives of the people. And whenever the public exigencies 
require that the property of any individual should be appropriated to 
public uses, he shall receive a reasonable compensation therefor.
text_haw:
[Pauku 15] . Pono no ke hoomalu pono ia kela kanaka 
keia kanaka ma na mea maikai o kona ola, a o kona wai- 
wai a o kona keakea kumu ole ia'i paha, e like me na 
Kanawai paa; a no ia mea, e pano no ia ia ke kokua i k^ 
kauwahi hapa kupono o kona waiwai, i mea e mau ai ua 
U-aluhia nei, a i ole ia, o kana hana ponoi, ke pono; aka 
aole loa e hiki ke laweia ke kauwahi lihi iki o ka w^aiwai 
o kekahi kanaka, aole hoi e haawiia i mea e pono ai ke 
Aupuni roe kona ae ole, a i ole ia, me ka ae ole o ka Moi, 
a me na'Lii, a me ka Poeikohoia e na Makaainana; 
a ina e laweia i kekahi manawa ka w^aiwai o kekahi ka- 
naka no ka pilikia o ke Aupuni, e loaa no ia ia ka uku ku- 
pono no ua waiwai nei.

text_en:
[Art. 16] No subsidy, impost, duties or tax of any description, shall 
be established or levied, nor any money drawn from the public treasury 
under any pretext whatsoever, without the consent of both branches of 
the legislature; provided that the Legislature shall make provision, in 
the annual bills of appropriation, for the emergency of war, invasion, 
or rebellion; and the Minister of Finance shall render a detailed ac- 
count to the Legislature of any expenditure made under that provision.
text_haw:
[Pauku 16] . Aole loa e kaulia kekahi auhau, a o keka- 
bi dute paha, aole hoi e unuhi iki ia'e kekahi dala noloko 
mai ka waihona dala Aupuni, me ka ae ole o na Hale 
elua o ka Ahaolelo o ke Aupuni; aka hoi e hoomaknukaii 
ka Ahaolek) roa ka bila Kalaiwaiwai inakahiki, i dala e 
pono ai ke Aupuni ke hiki i ka wa kaua huli moana, a kaua 
kuloko paha, a na ke Kuhioa Waiwai e hoike nui imua o 
ka Aha€(lelo, no na dala i Hlo malaila.

text_en:
[Art. 17] All retrospective laws are unjust; therefore, no such laws 
shall ever be passed.
text_haw:
[Pauku 17] . Aole no e kaulia kekahi Kanawai i mea e 
hoopili ai i kekahi hana i hala mamua; ua hewa ia a he 
heokaumaha wale.

text_en:
[Art. 18] The Military shall always be subject to the laws of the 
land; and no soldier shall, in time of peace, be quartered in any house, 
without the consent of the owner; nor in time of war, but in a manner 
to be prescribed by the Legislature.
text_haw:
[Pauku 18] . E noho no na mea ano koa malalo iho o na 
Kanawai o ka Aina; aole loa e hoonohoia kekahi koa ma 
kekahi kaie i ka wa malu me ko ka mea hale ae ole; aole 
hoi 1 ka wa kaua me ke kue i ke Kanawai a ka Ahaolelo 
e kau ai no ia mea.

text_en: [Art. 19] All elections by the people shall be by ballot.
text_haw:
[Pauku 19] . £ koho no na kanaka a pau ma na mea a 
ke Kanawai i haawi ai, ma ka faalota.

text_en:
[Art. 20] Every elector shall be privileged from arrest on election 
^ays, during his attendance at election, and in going to and returning 
therefrom, except in cases of treason, felony, or breach of the peace.
text_haw:
[Pauku 20] . Aole no e hopula kekahi mea koho ma na 
la koho oiai kona noho ana ma ia hana koho; a pela hoi i 
kona hele ana oiai, a i kona hoi ana aku mai kahi o ke 
koh<> ana, aole ia e hopuia, koe nae ke kipi, ka feloni, a 
me ka hoohauaaele.

text_en:
[Art. 21] No elector shall be so obliged to perform military duty, on 
the day of election, as to prevent his voting, except in time oi war or 
public danger.
text_haw:
[Pauku 21] . Aole e koiia kekahi mea koho e noho ana 
ma ka haiui koa i ka la koho a hiki ole ia ia ke koho, ke 
4)\e ia he wa kaua, a he wa pilikia paha o ke Aupuni. 
o ke ano o ke aupuni.

text_en:
[Art. 24] The King shall continue to he the supreme Executive 
Magistrate of this Kingdom under the title of His Majesty.
text_haw:
[Pauku 24] . ke Alii nui ka Luna kiekie hooko o keia 
Aupuni, a e kapaia oia o ka Moi.

text_en:
[Art. 25] The crown is herehy permanently confirmed to His Majes- 
ty Kamehameha III. during his life, and to his successors. The suc- 
cessor shall he the person whom the King and the House of Nobles 
shall appoint and puhlicly proclaim as such, during the King's life; 
hut should there be no such appointment and proclamation, then the 
successor shall be chosen by the House of Nobles and the House of 
Representfitives in joint ballot.
text_haw:
[Pauku 25] . E hoomau loa ia ka noho alii i ka Moi ia 
KamehamehalII.^oiaikonaoIa a me konapoehooilinaaku. 
£fa ba liooilif>a; o ka mea r kohoia a i hookaulanaia hoil 
pela e ka Moi a me ka Hale Ahaolelo Alii, oiai e ola ana 
ka, Moi, oia ka hooilina Alii; aka, i ole i hanaia pela, alai- 
Ja na na'Lii a me ka Po^ikohoia i ka Ahaol>elo e koho i 
ka hooilina Alii me ka balota hui.

text_en:
[Art. 26] No person shall ever sit upon the throne who has been 
convicted of any infamous crime, or who is insane or an idiot* No 
person Bhall ever succeed to the crown, unless he he a descendant of 
the aboriginal stock of Aliis.
text_haw:
[Pauku 26] . Aole loa e noho ma ka noho Alii kekahi 
mea i hoaherwaia mamua no kekahi hewa ino nui, aole hoi 
kekahi pupule a hupo paha; aole hoi e lilo ko^ahi i hooi- 
lina o ka Moi ke ole oia no ka hanauna Alii maeii o Ha- 
waii nei.

text_en:
[Art. 27] The King is Commander in Chief of the Army and Navy, 
and of all other Military forces of the Kingdom by sea and land; and 
has full power by himself, or by any officer or officers, he may appoint, 
to train and govern such forces, as he may judge best for the defense 
and safety of the Kingdom. But he shall never proclaim war without 
the consent of His Privy Council.
text_haw:
[Pauku 27] . Oke Aliika Alihikauamalunaanakema^me 
na manuwa a me na mea kaua e ae ma ka moana ai ma ka 
aina; a nona ka mana ma ona iho^ a ma o kekafM Lu»a 
.koa, a mau Luna paha ana c kobo ai, e ao a c hoopoiio^ 
pono i ua noau mea kaua nei, mamuli o koaa imtmy e po^ 
no a e malu ai ke Aupuni. Aka, aole^.e pono ia ia ka hapai 
i ke kaua me ka ae ole o kona Ahakukakuka Malu.

text_en:
[Art. 30] The King has the power, by and with the advice of His 
Privy Council, to make treaties, and appoint Ambassadors, other pub- 
Digitized by 
Google 
lie Ministers and Consult who shall be commissioned, accredited and 
instructed agreeably to the usage and laws of nations.
text_haw:
[Pauku 30] . Na ke Alii no, c kukakuka pu ana me ko-^ 
na Ahakukakuka Malu, e hana i na Kuikahi, a e koho f 
ika Luna Alii, i na Kauwa Aupuni e ae, a me na Kanike* 
)e, e hoounaia aku, a e haawiia ka lakou palapala hookow 
Au a me na kauoha e like me ke Eanawai o na Aupuoi.

text_en:
[Art. 32] He has the power, by and with the advice of His Cabi- 
net, and the approval of His Privy Council, to appoint and remove at 
His pleasure any of the several heads of the Executive Departments, 
and he may require information in writing from any of the officers in 
the Executive Departments, upon any subject relating to the duties of 
their respective offices.
text_haw:
[Pauku 32] . Nana no e kukakuka pu ana me kona Aliaku - 
hina a me kona Ahakukakuka Malu, e koho i kekahi o na 
poo a i kekahi mau poo paha o na Oihana hooko, a e hoo- 
pau aku ia lakou e like me kona manao; pono no hoi ia 
ia ke kauoha aku i na Luna a pau ma na Oihana hooko^ 
e hoike imua ona ma ka palapala, i na mea i pili i ka la- 
kou Oihana.

text_en:
[Art. 33] It is his duty to see that the Treaties and Laws of the land 
are faithfully observed and executed.
text_haw:
[Pauku 33] . Nana no e nana i ka malama pono ia a i ka 
hookoia o na Kuikahi a me na Kanawai o ka Aina.

text_en:
[Art. 34] The King has the power from time to time, to assemble 
His Cabinet or Privy Councillors to advise with him agreeably with 
the Constitution and Laws of the land.
text_haw:
[Pauku 34] . Na ka Moi e hoakoakoa, i kela manawa i 
keia manawa, i kona Ahakuhina a Ahakukakuka Malu pa- 
ha, e kukakuka pu me ia, e like me ke Kumukanawai a 
me na Kanawai o ka Aina.

text_en:
[Art. 35] The person of the King is inviolable and sacred; His Min- 
isters are responsible ; to the King belongs the Executive power; all 
laws that have passed both Houses of the Legislature, for their valid- 
ity, shall be signed by His Majesty and the Kuhina Nui; all his other 
official acts shall be approved by the Privy Council, countersigned by 
the Kuhina Nui, and by the Minister to whose Department such act 
may belong.
text_haw:
[Pauku 35] . He kapu loa ke kino o ke Alii nui a nie ka 
maluhia; maluna o na Kuhina kahihiao naOihana. Naka 
Moi ka mana hooko; e kakauia ka inoā o ka Moi ma na 
Kanawai a pau i hooholoia e na Hale elua o ka Ahaolelo, 
a e klikau pu ia ka inoa o ke Kuhinanui, a o kana mau 
hana e ae a pau ma ka Oihana e aeia e ka Ahakukakuka 
Malu, e kakau pu ia ka inoa o ke Kuhinanui, a me ka 
inoa pu hoi o ke Kuhina nana ia hana. 
. PAtrKu 36. . ka Moi, oia ke Alii nui maluna ona'Lii 
a me na kanaka a pau. Nona no ke Aupuni. 
• PiuKU 37. na inoa hanohano a pau a'me ka nani, a 
m% ka hooalii ana, mai ka Moi mai no lakoii.

text_en:
[Art. 38] The King coins money and regulates the currency by 
law.
text_haw:
[Pauku 38] . Na ka Moi no e hana i ke (lala, a e hoopo* 
liopono i ke dala ma ke Kanawai.

text_en:
[Art. 39] The King, by and with the approval of His Cabinet and 
Privy Council, in case of invasion or rebellion, can place the whole 
Kingdom, or any part of it under martial law; and he can even alien- 
ate it, if indispensable to free it from the insult and oppression of any 
foreign power.
text_haw:
[Pauku 39] . E hiki no i ka Moi me ka ae pu o kona 
Ahakuhina a me ka Ahakukakuka Malu i ka wa i kauia 
jBai) a haunaele paha no ke kipi, ke hoolilo i kona Aupu- 
ni a pau, a i kekahi hapa paha, malalo o ke Kanawai koa; 
a e hiki no hoi ia ia ke haawi loa aku i kona Aupuni, ke 
maopopo, oia ka mea e pau ai ka hoinoia mai a me ka 
hookaumahaia mai e kekahi Aupuni e ae.

text_en:
[Art. 40] The King's Standard and the National Ensign are main- 
tained as now established. 
Digitized by

8 CONSTITUTION.
text_haw:
[Pauku 40] . Ua hoomauia ka Hae o ka Moi, a me ka 
hae o ke Aupuni, e Uke me keia wa e noho nei. 
¥^AUKU 41. Ua kapu loa ko ka Moi mau aina ponoi a 
me kona waiwai e ae.

text_en:
[Art. 42] The King cannot be sued or held to account in any court 
or tribunal of the Realm.
text_haw:
[Pauku 42] . Aole no e hiki ke hoopiiia a hookolokoloia 
ka Moi iloko o kekahi Ahahookolokolo o ke Aupuni.

text_en:
[Art. 43] The King appoints some chief of rank and ability to be his 
Kuhina Nui, who shall be styled the Kuhina Nui of the Hawaiian Is- 
lands, and whose title shall be Highness.
text_haw:
[Pauku 43] . Na ka Moi no e koho i kekahi Aliihanoha- 
tio a me ka makaukau, e noho i Kuhinanui, a e kapaia 
oia *'o ke Kuhinanui o ko Hawaii Pae Aina;" a o kona 
inoa Oihana o ka Mea Kiekie.

text_en:
[Art. 44] The Kuhina Nui shall be the King'^s special Counsellor in 
the great afiairs of the Kingdom. All business connected with the 
special interests of the Kingdom, which the King wishes to transact, 
shall be done by the Kuhina Nui under the authority of the King. All 
Acts, Royal Patents, Commissions, and other official documents, duly 
executed by the Kuhina Nui in the name and by the consent of the 
King, agreeably with article 35, unless specially excepted by law, shall 
be equally binding as if executed by the King himself.
text_haw:
[Pauku 44] . ke Kuhinnnui ka hoa kuka pu o ka Moi 
tna na mea nui o ke Aupuui. na mea a pau e pili ana 
i ke Aupuni, a ka Moi e manao ai e hana, na ke Kuhina- 
nui no e hana ma ka inoa o ka Moi. O na hila, palapa^a 
sila nui, palapala hookohu, a nie na palapala Aupuni e ae 
i hana pono ia e ke Kuhinanui, ma ka inoa a me ka ae o 
ka Moi e like me ka olelo o ka pauku 35, ua like no ia 
mau mea me na mea a ka Moi i hana'i, koe nae na mea i 
hoakakaia ma ke Kanawai.

text_en:
[Art. 45] All important business of the Kingdom which the King 
chooses to transact in person, he may do, but not without the appro- 
bation of the Kuhina Nui. The King and Kuhina Nui shall have a 
negative on each other's public acts.
text_haw:
[Pauku 45] . E hiki no i ka Moi wale no ke hana i na 
hana nui a pau o ke Aupuni, ke makemake oia pela, me 
ka ae pu no nae o ke Kuhinanui. £ hiki no ia laua elua 
ke hoole kekahi i kekahi ma na hana a ke Aupuni.

text_en:
[Art. 46] The Kuhina Nui shall have charge of the Great Seal of 
the Kingdom, of the Royal Standard, and of the National Flag; and 
in the absence of the King, he shall preside over the deliberations of 
the Privy Council.
text_haw:
[Pauku 46] . Na ke Kuhinanui, e malama i ka Sila nui o 
ke Aupuni, i ka hae o ka Moi a me ko ke Aupuni; a na- 
na no hoi e noho Peresidena o ka Ahakukakuka Malu, 
m 
ke ole ka Moi.

text_en:
[Art. 49] There shall contmue to be a Council of State for advising 
the King in the Executive part of the Government, and in directing 
the afiairs of the Kingdom, according to the Constitution and laws of 
the land , to be called the King's Privy Council of Stale,
text_haw:
[Pauku 49] . E mau no ka Ahakukakuka Malu o ke Au- 
puni, e imi pu me ke Alii nui ma na Oihana Uooko o k^ 
Aupuni, a ma ka hoopōnopono ana i ko ke Aupuni, e like' 
roe ke Kumukanawai a me na Kanawai o ka Aina, a e 
kapaia oia, o ko ka Moi Ahakukakuka Malu o ke Aupuni.

text_en:
[Art. 50] The members of the Privy Council are appointed by the 
King, and hold their offices during His Majesty's pleasure. The 
King's Ministers and the Governors of the Islands are, ex officio, mem- 
bers of His Privy Council. The Privy Council regulates its own pro- 
ceedings by fiye-laws enacted by themselves and approved by the 
King.
text_haw:
[Pauku 50] . Na ka Moi e koho i na mea komo iloko a 
ka Ahakukakuka Malu, a e noho iakou oiai ua kupono i 
kona manao. Ma ka hikou oihana ke komo ana o na Ku- 
hina, a me na Kiaaina iloko o ka Ahakukakuka Malu. 
Na ka Ahakukakuka Malu e kau i na Kai^wai no ka la- 
kou hana ana, me ka ae o ka Moi.

text_en:
[Art. 54] Each of them shall make an annual report to the Legisla- 
ture, made up to the first of January next preceding, of the transac- 
tions and business of his Department, within one week after the open- 
ing of the Legislature.
text_haw:
[Pauku 54] . E hoike kela mea keia mea o lakou, im*ia 
o ka Ahaolelo, i kela makahiki keia makahiki, a hiki i ka 
la mua o Ianuari mamua iho, i ka hana a kana oihana ilo- 
ko o ka makahiki i hala aku, iloko o ka hebedoma hooka-^ 
hi mahope iho o ka akoakoa ana o ka Ahaolelo.

text_en:
[Art. 57] The King with the consent of the Governor, may appoint 
in one or more islands, a Lieutenant Governor, during His pleasure, 
to assist the Governor but always subordinate to him in authority.
text_haw:
[Pauku 57] . E hiki no i ke Alii, me ka ae o ke Kiaaina, 
ke koho ma keka^ii Mokupuni, a mau Mokupuni paha, i 
Kokua Kiaaina, oiai ua kupono i ko k*^ Moi makemake, e 
hana pu me ke Kiaaina, malalo iho nae ona.

text_en:
[Art. 60] The Legislative Power of this Kingdom is vested in the 
King, the House of Nobles, and the House of Representatives; each 
of whom has a negative on the other.
text_haw:
[Pauku 60] . O ka Mana kau Kanawai o keia Aupuni, 
Aia no i ka Moi, me ka Hale Ahaolelo Alii, me ka Hale o 
ka Poeikohoia o na M^kaainana, a e hiki no ia lakou ke 
hoole kekaiii i k^kalii.

text_en:
[Art. 61] The Legislative Body shall assemble annually, for the 
purpose of seeking the welfare of the nation, in the first week in April, 
and at such other time, and in the place that the King may judge 
necessary. This Body shall be styled the Legislature of the Hawaiian 
Islands.
text_haw:
[Pauku 61] . Na ke AHr no e koho a e hookohu i llona' 
mau Kuhina, a e noho iakou ma ka lakou Oihana, oiai ks 
manao o ka Moi pela; e hiki nae ke hoopii Luna nui ia.

text_en:
[Art. 62] Full power and authority are hereby given to said Legis- 
lature, from time to time, to make all manner of wholesome laws, either 
with penalties or without, as they shall judge to be for the welfare of 
the nation, and for the necessary support and defense of good govern* 
Kent; provided the same be not repugnant or contrary to this Consti- 
tution.
text_haw:
[Pauku 62] . na Kuhi^a o ke Alii he Ahakuhina ka 
lakou, a ma ia ano he poe hoakuka pu ponoi lakou no ka^ 
Moi, ma na hana hooko o ke Aupuni.

text_en:
[Art. 63] No bill or resolution, although it may have passed the 
Legislature, shall become a law, or have force as such, until it shall 
have been presented to the King, through the Kubina Nui, for bis r^ 
Digitized by 
Google 
visali and if he approve thereof, he shall signify his approbation by 
fiigDing the same. But if he have any objection to the passing of 
such bill or resolve, he shall return it with his objections in writing to 
that House in which it shall have originated, who shall enter the ob- 
jections at large on their journal, and xko such bill shall be brought for- 
ward thereafter during the same session.
text_haw:
[Pauku 63] . E noho lakou a pau raa kahr e noho ai ke 
Aupuni; a e malama pakahi lakou i ka hana a kona mau 
lalo a me na Kakauolelo; a ina i kupono i ka hanohano o- 
ka Moi a me ka hana a ke Aupuni, e haawi i kekahi o aa 
Hale Ahaolelo, i na olelo hoakaka no ka lakou Oihana, a 
e hele no oia, a o kona lalo paha, imua o lakou, e like 
me ko lakou manao pakahi.

text_en:
[Art. 64] Each House shall be the judge of the qualifications of its 
own members, and a majority of each shall constitute a quorum to do 
business; but a smaller number may adjourn from day to day, and 
compel the attendance of absent members, in such manner and under 
such penalties as each House may provide.
text_haw:
[Pauku 64] . Na kela Ilale, keia Hale, e hooponopono i 
ka noho ana o kona poe ponoi; a na ka hapa nui e hooho- 
lo i na hana a pau; akn, e hiki no i ka hapa uuku ke hoo- 
panee i ka halawai i kela la i keia la, a e koi aku no hoi 
i na mea i hiki ole mai e akoakoa pu me lakou, mamuli o 
ka hana, a me ka hoopai, i hooholoia e ua Hale la. 
Pauku G5. Na kela Hale, keia Hale, e koho i kona 
mau Luna, a e kau i na Kanawai e holo ai ka lakou maa 
hana.

text_en:
[Art. 66] Each House shall have authority to punish by imprison- 
ment, not exceeding thirty days, every person, not a member, who 
shall be guilty of disrespect to the House, by any disorderly or con- 
temptuous behavior in its presence; or who during the time of its sit- 
ting, shall threaten harm to the body or estate of any of its members^ 
for any thing said or done in the House, or who shall assault any of 
4hem therefor, or who shall assault or arrest any witness or other per- 
■son ordered to attend the House, in his way going or returning; or 
who shall rescue any person arrested by order of the House.
text_haw:
[Pauku 66] . Na kela Hale, keia Hale, e hoopai, ma ka 
halepaahao, no na la aole e oi aku mamua o ke kanakolu^ 
i kela kanaka, keia kanaka, aole oia no lakou, ke hooma- 
ewaewa oia i ua Hale nei ma ka hana hoohaunaele, a hoo- 
wahawaha paha, iloko ona; a ke howeliweli paha oia i 
kekahi o ua Hale nei, ma ka hana ino i kona kino, a i ka- 
na waiwai paha, oiai e noho akoakoa ana ko ia Hale, no 
kekahi mea ana i hana^i, a olelo ai paha, iloko o ua Hale 
nei; a ke lele ino paha oia i kekahi o ua poe la no ua mau- 
mea nei; a ke lele ino, a hopu paha oia i kekahi hoike, a 
i kekahi kanaka e ae paha, i kauohaia e ka Hale, i kona 
hele ana ilaila, a hoi ana paha; a ke hoopakele paha oia i 
kekahi kanaka i hopuia mamuli o ke kauoha a ua Halē la.

text_en:
[Art. 67] Each House may punish its own members for disorderly 
behavior.
text_haw:
[Pauku 67] . Na kela Hale, keia Hale e hoopai i kona 
poe ponoi no na hana hoohaunaele.

text_en:
[Art. 68] Each House shall keep a journal of its proceedings, and 
the yeas and nays of the members of either House, on any question, 
ishall, at the desire of one Mb of those present, be entered on the jour- 
nal.
text_haw:
[Pauku 68] . E malama kela Hale keia Hale i Buke 
mooolelo no ka lakou mau hana, a ina e makemake ka ha- 
pa lima hookahi o ka poe e noho ana i kekahi Hale, alaila 
e kakauia, iloko o ua buke nei, na inoa o ka poe ae a me 
poe hoole o ka Hale i kel?i hana i keia hana.

text_en:
[Art. 69] The members of either House shall in all cases, except 
^treason, felony or breach of the peace, be privileged from arrest during 
their attendance at the session of their respective houses, and in going 
to and returning from the same ; and they shall not be held to answer 
for any speech, or debate made in the House, in any other court or 
f>1ace whatsoever.
text_haw:
[Pauku 69] . Aole loa e hopuia kekahi o ka poe Ahao- 
13 
leIOy oiai e noho ana oia mamuli o ia Aha, a c he!e ana ilai- 
)a, ā e hoi ana aku paha, koe nae ke kipi, ka feIoni, a me 
kit hoohaunaele; aole hoi lakou e hookolokoloia imua o 
kekahi Ahahookolokolo e, a i kau wahi e paha, no keka- 
hi alelo, a olelo paio paha malaila.

text_en:
[Art. 70] The Members of the House of Representatives shall re- 
ceive, for their services a compensation to be ascertained by law and 
imid oQt of the public treasury^ but no increase of compensation shall 
Digitized by 
Google 
12 CONSTITUTION. 
take effeet during the year in which it shall have been made; and no 
law shall be passed, increasing the conipensaiion of said members be- 
yond the sum of three dollars per day. 
Art. 7J. The members of the House of Nobles sit without pay; but 
they may receive hereafter such a compensation as the law may enact.
text_haw:
[Pauku 70] . E ukuia ka Poeikohoia e na Makaainana; 
no ka lakou hana,.e like Rie ka mea i oleloia ma ke Ka- 
nawai, noloko ae o ka waihona dala Aupuni; aole nae e 
hoonuiia keia uku i ka makahiki i hooholoia ka olelo e hoo« 
Bui i ka uku; aole hoi e kaulia kekahi Kanawai e heouia- 
huahua ana i ka uku o ua poe la, mamua o na dala ekolu 
no ka la.

[Pauku 72] . Na ke Alii no e koho i ka poe o ka Hale 
^haolelo Alii, a e noho lakou ma ia Oihana, a pāu ko la- 
kou ola, e like nae me ka Pauku 67» aole nae e oi aku k6 
lakou nui mamua o ke kanakolu. 
, Paūku 73. Aole e kohoia kekahi e noho ma ka Hale 
Ahaolelo Alii, ke hiki olē aku oia i na makahiki he iwa^ 
kaluakumamakahi, a ke ole hoi oia i noho i na makahiki 
^\\tAh ma keia Aupuni.

text_en:
[Art. 74] The House of Nobles shall be a Court with full and sole 
authority to hear and determine all impeachments made by the House 
of Representatives, against any officer or officers o( the Kingdom, for 
mis-conduct and mal-administration in their offices; but previous to 
the trial of every impeachment, the members shall respectively be 
sworn, truly and impartially to try and determine the charge in ques- 
tion, according to evidence and the law. Their judgment, however, 
shall not extend further than to removal from office and disqualification 
to hold or enjoy any place of honor, trust, or profit under this govern- 
ment; but the party so convicted shall be, nevertheless, liable to indict- 
ment, trial, judgment, and punishment, according to the laws of the 
land.
text_haw:
[Pauku 74] . E noho ka Hale Ahaolelo Alii i Ahahooko- 
lokolb» no lakou wale no ka manā a pau e hoolohe ^ e 
ll0o[)onoj!ioiio i na hoopii nui a pau i hoōpiiia'i e ka Hale o 
kd PoeiUohoia, i kekahi Luiia nui, a mau Luna nui paha^ 
ifb ka lakou oihana aka» mamuā aku o ka hookolokolo i 
keld hoopii nui, keia hoopii nui, e hoohiki pakahi keia poe 
e hookolokolo pono me ka ewaewa ole i ka mea i hoopii- 
ia'i, mamuli o ka na hoike a me ke Kanawai. Aole nae 
e oi aka kft lakou olelo hoahewa i ka hoopau i kona noho 
Luila jitiaj ā me ka hoole loa i kona noho hou ana ma ke- 
kiEihi Oihana e hanohano ai a e waiwai ai paha, malalo iho 
a k0ia Aupuni; aka e hiki no i ka mea i hoahewaia pela^ 
ke hodpii hou ia, a e hookolokolo ia, a e hoahewa hou ia^ 
a e hoopai hou ia mahope maniuli o ke Kanawai o ka 
Aina. 
14

text_en:
[Art. 75] The House of Representatives shall be composed of not 
less than twenty-four nor more than forty members, who shall be elec- 
ted annually.
text_haw:
[Pauku 75] . Aole e oi aku ka nui o ka Hale o ka Poei^ 
kohoja mamua o ke kanaha, aole hoi e emi mai malalo o 
ka ])yakaluakumainaha, a e kohoia lakou i kela makahiki 
i keia makahiki.

text_en:
[Art. 76] The Representation of the people shall be based on the 
principle of equality, and shall be forever regulated and apportioned 
according to the population, to be ascertained by the official census. 
In the year one thousand eight hundred and fifty-three, and every 
sixth year thereafter, the number of Representatives shall be fixed by 
the Legislature agreeably with this and the preceding article.
text_haw:
[Pauku 76] . £ mahele like ia ka Poeikohoia o na Ma- 
kaainana, a e hooponopono mau loa ia mamuli o ka nui o 
na kanaka i hoomaopopoia ma ko ke Aupuni helu ana. I 
ka makahiki hookahi tausani ewalu haneri me kanalima* 
kumamakolu, a i ka paono o na makahiki ma ia hope aku, 
e iiooponoponoia ka nui o ka Hale o ka Poeikohoia ma*- 
muli o keia a me ka Pauku maiuna iho, e ka Ahaolelo.

text_en:
[Art. 77] No person shall be eligible for a Representative of the 
people, who is insane, or an idiot, or who shall at any time have been 
convicted of any infamous crime, nor unless he be a male subject or 
Digitized by 
Google 
denizen of the Kingdom, who shall have arrived at the full age of 
twenty-five years, who shall know how to read and write, who shall 
understand accounts, and who shall have resided in the Kingdom for at 
least one year immediately preceding his election.
text_haw:
[Pauku 77] . Aole no e kohoia kekahi no ka Hale o ka 
Poeikohoia, ina ua pupule, a hupo paha, a ua hihia i ke 
karain)a nui ma ka hookolokolo ana, a ke ole ia he kana- 
ka malalo o ka Moi, a i loaa ole paha ia ia ka palapaia 
hookupa, a ke hiki ole aku oia i na makahiki he iwakalua- 
k^ioam^alima, a ke ike oLe hoi i ka heluhelu a me ke ka- 
kaulima» ke maopopo ole hoi ia ia ka helu, a i ole hoi oia 
i noho ma keia Aupuni i hookahi makahiki mamiia iho p 
kona kohoia ana.

text_en:
[Art. 78] Every male sobject of His Majesty, whether native or nat- 
uralized, and every denizen of the Kingdom, who shall have paid his 
taxes, who shall have attained the full age of twenty years, and who 
shall have resided in the Kingdom for one year immediately preceding 
the time of election, shall be entitled to one vote for the representative 
or representatives, of the district in which he may have resided three 
months next preceding the day of election ; provided that no insane 
person, nor any person who shall at any lime have been convicted of 
any infamous crime, within this Kingdom, unless he shall have been 
pardoned by the King, and by the terms of such pardon been restored 
to all the rights of a subject, shall be allowed to vote.
text_haw:
[Pauku 78] . Q kela mea keia mea o na kanaka maoli, 
me na haole hoohiki, a me na faao!e i loaa ka palapala hoo- 
kupa, ina ua hookaaia kona auhau, a ina ua hiki aku oia i 
na makahiki he iwakalua, a ina ua noho oia ma keia Au- 
puni i hookahi makahiki mamua iho o ka la koho, e hiki no 
ia ia ke koho me ka balota hookahi i kekahi kanaka, a 
mau kanaka paha o ka Hale o ka Poeikohoia no ka Apana 
kahi ana i noho ai no na malama ekolu mamua iho o ka la 
koho; aka, aole e hiki i kekahi kanaka pupule ke koho^ 
aole hoi i kekahi kanaka i hihia i ke karaima nui ma ka 
hookolokolo ana, ma keia Aupuni, ke ole i kala e ia kona 
hewa e ka Moi, a ke ole hoi i loaa ia ia na pono a pau o 
ke kanaka kupa ma ia kala ana.

text_en:
[Art. 79] All bills or resolves for raising the revenue, or calling for 
any expenditure of the public money, shall originate in the House of 
Representatives; but the House of Nobles may propose or concur with 
amendments as on other bills.
text_haw:
[Pauku 79] . Na ka Hale o ka Poeikohoia e hapai mua i 
na bila a pau a me na olelo e hooholoia no ka hookupu 
lana a me ka uku ana aku i ke dala o ke Aupuni; e hiki 
no nae i ka Hale Ahaolelo Alii ke hoololi hou ae, a ke ae 
I ka hoololi hou ana ae paha i ua mau bi)a nei, e ]ike me 
oa hila e ae. 
l^AUKU 80. E noho no ka Hale o ka Poeikohoia i ata 
hinaninau nui o ke Aupuni, a e hoolohe ia a hookolokoloia 
ho hoi e ka llale Ahaolelo Alii ha hooph Luna nui a pau i 
hanaia e lakou. 
O KA OIHANA HOO^^OLOKOLŌ

text_en:
[Art. 81] The Judicial Power of the Kingdom shall be vested in one 
Supreme Court, and in such inferior courts as the Legislature may 
firom time to time establish.
text_haw:
[Pauku 81] . E pili ka mana o ka Oihana Hookolokolo 
iloko o ka Ahahookolokolo Kiekie hookahi, a me na Aha- 
hookolokolo malalo iho i hoonohnohooia i kela manav<'a i 
keia manawa e ka Ahaolelo.

text_en:
[Art. 82] The Supreme Court shall eonsist of a Chief Justice and 
two associate Justices, any of whom may hold the court. The Justi- 
ces of the Supreme Court shall hold their offices during good behavior^ 
subject to removal upon impeachment; and shall, at stated times, re- 
ceive for their services a compensation, which ehaU not be diminished 
during their continuance in office. Provided, however, that any judge 
ef the Supreme Court, or of any other Court of Record, may be re- 
moved from office for mental or physical inability by a concurrent 
resolution of two-thirds of both branches of the Legislature. The 
judge against whom the Legislature may be about to proceed, shall re- 
Digitized by 
Google 
14 COHSTITUTIOK. 
ceive Dotice thereof, accompanied by a copy of the causes alledged for 
bis removal, at least twenty days before the day oo which either branch 
of the Legislature shall act thereon.
text_haw:
[Pauku 82] . Ma ka Ahahookolokolo Kiekie e hoonohoia 
kekahi Lunākanawai Kiekie a me na Lunakanawai Kokmi 
elua, a e hiki i kekahi o lakou e noho a e hooponopono i 
kekahi halawai o ua Aha nei. 
E paa na Lunakanawai o ka Ahahookolokolo Kiekie i 
ka lakou oihana, oiai e pono ka lakou hana ana^ e pili nae 
ia lakou ka hoopii luna nui; a e loaa ia lakou, i ka wamae- 
po, ke kauwahi uku, no ka lakou hana^ a oiai lakou i paa i 
keia oihana aole e hoemiiā ua uku nei; akā hoi, e hiki ke ki- 
pakuia kekahi Lunakawai o ka Ahakiekie, ā o keka&i 
Ahakakau paha, no ka hemahei^a o kona kino, a o kōAa 
manao paha, ke hooholoia pela e ha hapakolu elua o na 
Hale elua o ka Ahaolelo; e lohe mua no nae ka Lunakā- 
tiawai i ka hoopi'i imua o ka Ahaolelo, a e lilo ia ia ke ko« 
pe o na kuma hoopii, he iwakalua la mamua o ka hana afte 
imua kekahi Hale Ahaolelo.

text_en:
[Art. 83] The Kingdom shall be divided, by law, into a convenient 
number of circuits, not less than four, nor exceeding eight, subject to 
alteration by the Legislature, from time to time, as the public good may 
require; for each of which one or more Circuit Judges, not exceeding 
three, however, shall be .appointed to hold their offices during good 
behavior, subject to removal upon impeachment.
text_haw:
[Pauku 83] . E maheleia ke Aupuni, ma ke Kanawai, i 
<ia Moku e poho ai, aole nae e'emi i eha, aole hoi ē pi 
āku i ewalu, a na ka Ahaolelo ia e hooponopono i kela 
manawa i keia manawa, e like me ka hiea kupono i ka lē- 
{i'ulehu, a no ua mau Meku nei e hoonohoia pakahi, a oi 
āku paha, nfa Lunakanawai Kaapuni, aole nae e oi aku i 
^kolu, a e paa keia oihana ia lakou oiai ua pono ka lakem 
hana ana, a( er hiki no nae ke hoopii lona nui ra. '

text_en:
[Art. 84] The judicial power shall extend to all cases in Law and 
Equity, arising under the Constitution, any law of this Kingdom, and 
treaties made, or which shall be made under their authority; to all 
cases affecting Ambassadors, other public ministers and Consuls, and 
to all cases of Admiralty and maratime jurisdiction.
text_haw:
[Pauku 84] . E prH ka mana o ka Oihana Hookolokolo? i 
Ha hana a pau ma ke Kanawai a me ke Kaulike, malalo iho 
o ke Kumukanawai a o kekahi Kanawai, o keia Aupuai, 
ā me na Kuikahi i hanaia, a e hanaia ana paha malalo iho 
ko lakou ma»a; a i na hana a pau e pili ana i na Luna 
Alii kiekie, a me na Kauwa Aupuni e ae a me na Kani* 
16 
kele, a i na hana hoi e pili ana i ka oihana hooko)oko)(> 
moku a me ka hoomalu moana.

text_en:
[Art. 85] The Judicial power shall be divided among the Supreme 
Court and the several inferior courts of the Kingdom, in such manner 
as the Legislature may from time to time indicate.
text_haw:
[Pauku 85] . E maheleia ka mana o ka Oihana Hookolo- 
kolo iwaena o ka Ahahookolokolo Kiekie a me na Ahahoo- 
kolokolo e ae o ke Aupuni malalo iho, e like me ke kuhi- 
kuhi ana o ka Ahaolelo, i kela manawa i keia manawa.

text_en:
[Art. 86] The Chief Justice of the Supreme Court shall be the 
Chancellor of the Kingdom; he shall be ex-officio President of the House 
of Nobles in all cases of impeachment, unless when impeached himself; 
and exercise such jurisdiction in equity or other cases as the law may 
confer upon him, his decisions being subject, however, to the revision 
of the Supreme Court, on appeal.
text_haw:
[Pauku 86] . E noho no ka Lunakanawai Kiek!e i Luna- 
kanawai Kaulike o ke Aupuni, a e noho Peresidena oia o 
ka Hale x\haolelo Alii, oiai e hookolokoloia ana kekahi 
hoopii Luna Nui, ke ole oia ka niea i hoopiiia; a ia ia ka 
mana e hana ma ke Kaulike a ma na mea e ae, e like me 
ka haawi ana o ke Kanawai ia ia; aka e nana hou ia na 
mea i hooholoia e ia e na Lunakanawai o ka Ahahookolo^ 
kolo kiekie, ke hoopiiia ilaila. 
Paūku 87. E paa loa maluna o na aoao a pau na olelo 
i hooholoia ma ka Ahahookolokolo Kiekie, e ka nui o na 
LunakanaWai o ia Aha, aole hoopii hou aku.

text_en:
[Art. 88] The King, His Ministers, the Governors, and each branch 
of the Legislature shall have authority to require the opinions of the 
Justices of the Supreme Court, upon important questions of ]aw, and 
upon solemn occasions.
text_haw:
[Pauku 88] . E hiki no i ka Moi, i kona mau Kuhina, i 
&a ^iaaina, a me na Hale o ka Ahaolelo pakahi, ke kauo- 
jia i aa Lunakanawai o ka Ahahookolokolo Kiekie e hoi- 
j^i ko īakou manao no na hana nui ma ke Kanawai a me 
ua wa pilikia a hanohano nui.

text_en:
[Art. 92] No Judge or Magistrate can sit alone on an appeal or new 
trial, in any case on which he may have given a previous judgment.
text_haw:
[Pauku 92] . Aole no e noho a hookolokolo kekahi Lu- 
KakaAawai^ oia hookahi wale no, i kekahi mea hōopii imua 
ona, a i kekahi mea hou paha, ana i hookolokolo ai, a i 
hooholo ai mamua.

text_en:
[Art. 93] It shall be the duty of the Chief Justice to make an an- 
nual report, through the Minister of the Interior, to the Legislature, of 
the state of the Judiciary of the Kingdom in all its branches.
text_haw:
[Pauku 93] . Na ka Lunakanawai Kiekie e hoike imua o 
ka Ahaolelo, ma o ke Kuhina Nui la, i kela makahiki i 
keia makahiki i ka hana iloko o ka Oihana Hookolokolo» 
aa koaa mau aoao a pau.

text_en:
[Art. 94] The King, after approving this Constitution, shall take 
the following oath : 
I solemnly swear, in the presence of Almighty God, to maintain the 
Constitution of the Kingdom whole and inviolate, and to govern io 
conformity with that and the laws.
text_haw:
[Pauku 94] . Aia a aponoia keia Kumukanawai e ka 
Moi, e hoohiki oia penei: 
Ke hoohiki nei au, imua o ke Akua mana loa, e mala- 
ma loa au i ke Kumukanawai o keia Aupuni, a e hoopo- 
nopoAO no au i ke Aupuni mamuli o ke Kumukanawai a 
me na Kaaawai.

text_en:
[Art. 95] The Kuhina Nui shall take the same oath ; and when ex«- 
ereising the Executive Power, during a minority, he shall take the 
following oath : 
I solemnly swear, in the presence of Almighty God, to preserve the 
rights of the Heir to the Crown, and the Constitution whole and invi^ 
olate, and to govern in conformity with that and the law.
text_haw:
[Pauku 95] . £ hoohiki ke Kuhina Nui e like no me ka 
Moi; a ina e loaa ia ia ka mana hooko nui, oiai e noho 
keiki ana kekahi hooilina o ka noho Alii, alaila e hoohiki 
oia penei: 
Ke hoohiki nei au/imua o ke Akua mana loa, e mala- 
ma loa au i na pono a pau o ka hooilina nona ke Aupuni, 
a me ke Kumukanawai, a e hoomalu i ke Aupuni mamuli 
o ke Kumukanawai, a me na Kanawai. 
Paui(:u 96. £ hoohiki pakahi na 'Lii o ka Ahaolelo 
penei; 
Ke hoohiki nei au, imua o ke Akua mana loa, e kokua 
au mamuli o ke Kumukanawai a me na Kanawai o ko Ha- 
waii Pae Aina, a e hana pololei me ka ewaewa ole i ka'u 
hana iloko o keia Hale.

text_en:
[Art. 97] Every member of the House of Representatives shall 
take the following oath :

text_en:
[Art. 100] The Legislature votes the appropriations for the year, 
after due consideration of the revenue and expenditure of the year 
preceding, and of the estimates of the revenue and expenditure for the 
current year, which shall be submitted to them by the Minister of Fi* 
nance ; all which accounts shall be appended to the Bill of Appropria- 
tions and published annually.
text_haw:
[Pauku 100] . Ncj ka Ah^'Oielo e iiooholo i ka Bila Haa- 
wina no ka makahiki, mahope nae o ka noonoo pono ana 
i ka palapala a ke Kuhina Waiwai e hoike aku ai imoa o 
lakou; e hoakaka ana i ka loaa mai a me ka lilo aku o ha 
makahiki i hala, a me ia makahiki pu no; a e hoolaha pu 
ia ia mau palapala nie ka Eila Haawina i kela makahikii 
keia makahiki.

text_en:
[Art. 101] The enacting style in making and passing all Acts and 
Laws, shall be: — *'Be it enacted by the King, the Nobles and the Repre- 
sentatives of the Hawaiian Islands in Legislative Council assembled.^
text_haw:
[Pauku 101] . Penei e hooholoia'i na Bila a me na Ka- 
nawai a pau, *'E. h^oholoia e ke AUi me na 'Lii a me ka 
Poeikohoiu o ko Hawnii Pae Ain^a, i akoakoa iloko o ka 
Ahaolelo kau {{>anawai."

text_en:
[Art. 102] To avoid improper influences which may result from in- 
termixing in one and the same Act such things as have no proper re- 
lation to each other, every law shall embrace but one object, and that 
shall be expressed in the title.
text_haw:
[Pauku 102] . I mea e hihia ole ai, uo ka hui o na mea 
ano okoa iloko o ke Kanawai hookahi, e hookaawalieia ke 
kumu hookahi ma ke Kanawai'hookahi, a e hoakakaia ke 
kumu ma ka olelo mua.

text_en:
[Art. 103] All laws now in force in this Kingdom, shall continue 
and remain in full effect, until altered or repealed by the Legislature ; 
such parts only excepted as are repugnant to this Constitution. AU 
laws now enacted, or that may hereafter be enacted, contrary to this 
Constitution, shall be null and void.
text_haw:
[Pauku 103] . E mau no na lCanawar e kau nei', me tta 
mana mau, a hiki i ko'lakou hoololi hou ia ana, a hoopau 
ia ana paha e ka Ahaolelo Aupuni, koe nae na Kanawap 
kue i keia Ku-mukanawai E ole loa na Kanawai kue f 
keia Kumukanawai, o narKanawai e kau nei a O' na mea 
e hooholoia mahope f)aha.

text_en:
[Art. 104] This Constitution shall be in force from the first Monday 
of December in the year one thousand eight hundred and fifty-two ; 
but that there may be no failure of justice, or danger to the Kingdom, 
from any change, all officers of this Kingdom, at the time this Consti- 
tution shall take effect, shall have, hold, and exercise all the powers 
to them granted, until other persons shall be appointed in their stead ; 
Digitized by

and all courts of law shall proceed in the execution of the business of 
their respective departments ; and all executive and legislative officers, 
bodies and powers, shall continue in full force, in the enjoyment and 
exercise of their trusts, employments and authority, until new appoint- 
ments or elections shall take place ander this Constitution.

text_en:
[Art. 105] Any amendment or amendments to this constitution may 
be proposed in either branch of the legislature, and if the same shall 
be agreed to by a majority of the members of each house, such pro- . 
posed amendment or amendments shall be entered on their journals, 
with the yeas and nays taken thereon, and referred to the next legisla- 
ture ; which proposed amendment or amendments shall be published 
for tbre% months previous to the election of the next house of repre- 
8entati\es ;^and if, in the next legislature, such proposed amend- 
ment or amendments, shall be agreed to by two-thirds of all the mem- 
bers of each house, and be approved by the King, such amendment 
or amendments shall become part of the Constitution of this Kingdom.
